subject: Stack alignment in Imod tiltalign / autoalign_dynamo
body:
Hi! 

I'm processing a cryo-ET dataset following the teamtomo walkthrough. 

I'm preprocessing and generating the unaligned stacks in Warp. I have been 
using autoalign_dynamo for alignment and then reconstructed the tomograms 
in Warp. autoalign_dynamo selects all gold fiducials that it can find, but 
I would like to try to exclude those that are located on carbon to see if 
that improves the quality of the reconstructions. 

1.) Does anyone know if it's possible to add a mask for autoalign_dynamo to 
do bead selection - similar to the imod "use boundary model"? I've been 
going through the scripts but cannot find anything. 

My Plan B is to use etomo until tiltalign:
2a)  As far as I understand Warp only needs the .xf files generated from 
tiltalign to be able to reconstruct? 
2b) Is it "allowed" to refine global variables in etomo fine alignment, e.g 
Rotation, Magnification, Tilt Angle for Warp reconstruction to work 
optimally? i.e, will there be a problem if the refined tilt-angles in the 
taSolution.log are different from the "nominal" ones (those in .rawtilt)? 

Best,
Stefan
